description: Calling a dangerous-permission API without requesting it at runtime, causing a SecurityException crash.
body: Failure mode: the app uses a protected API (location, camera, contacts) assuming the permission is granted, but on modern Android dangerous permissions need a runtime grant, so the call throws SecurityException and crashes the feature. Detection signals: a crash with SecurityException when the feature first runs; the permission is declared in the manifest but never requested at runtime; works only after the user grants it elsewhere. Do not assume a dangerous permission is granted. Safer fallback: check the grant at point of use, request via the permissions launcher with a rationale when appropriate, handle denial and permanent-denial gracefully, and test all denial paths.
